policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate Risk and Sustainable Finance
policy.description: RBI released binding climate risk management guidelines for scheduled commercial banks, mandating climate stress testing by March 2027 and disclosure of physical/transition risk exposures. Banks with assets over ₹1 lakh crore must submit first climate scenario analysis reports by September 2026.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Top 500 Listed Companies
policy.description: SEBI extended mandatory BRSR Core reporting with reasonable assurance to top 500 companies by market capitalization, effective FY2026-27. New additions include Scope 3 emissions for material categories and water stress assessments for manufacturing facilities.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Exposure Draft on Index-Based Parametric Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI published draft regulations permitting insurers to offer parametric products tied to rainfall indices, temperature thresholds, and wind speed triggers. Draft proposes 72-hour payout timelines and allows banks to distribute parametric covers as bundled credit products.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee Finalizes Climate Pillar 3 Disclosure Framework
policy.description: BCBS finalized standardized climate disclosure requirements under Pillar 3, mandating banks to report financed emissions by sector and physical risk exposures by geography starting January 2028. Framework includes common metrics for transition and physical risk.

policy.title: ISSB Launches Emerging Markets Adoption Roadmap with India as Priority Jurisdiction
policy.description: IFRS Foundation announced a phased ISSB adoption program for 15 emerging markets, with India targeted for baseline S1/S2 alignment by 2028. MCA and SEBI named as coordinating bodies for interoperability with existing BRSR framework.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Granularity
policy.description: NGFS published updated scenarios incorporating IPCC AR6 data with sub-national physical risk projections for South Asia. New scenarios include acute hazard frequencies for India's coastal and agricultural regions through 2100.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Partners with HDFC Bank for Physical Risk Analytics
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a multi-year partnership with HDFC Bank to provide asset-level flood and cyclone risk scoring for commercial real estate portfolios. Deal reportedly valued at $4-5 million annually, marking Jupiter's first major Indian banking client.

competitors.title: Sprih Closes $18M Series B to Expand Climate Risk Module for Banks
competitors.description: Bangalore-based Sprih raised Series B led by Lightspeed India, with participation from existing investor Accel. Company announced plans to build physical risk overlays for loan portfolios targeting mid-sized NBFCs.

competitors.title: Moody's Launches Climate Risk-Adjusted PD Model for Emerging Market Banks
competitors.description: Moody's released a climate-adjusted probability of default model covering 22 emerging markets including India. Model integrates physical hazard data with credit fundamentals for corporate lending portfolios above $10 million exposure.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory Signs Framework Agreement with State Bank of India
competitors.description: GIST Advisory entered a framework agreement with SBI to provide natural capital and biodiversity risk assessments for agricultural lending. Pilot covers 50,000 farmer accounts across three water-stressed districts in Maharashtra.

competitors.title: XDI Releases India-Specific Physical Risk Database with Taluk-Level Resolution
competitors.description: Australian climate analytics firm XDI launched an India physical risk dataset covering 5,800 taluks with projections for riverine flooding, heat stress, and cyclonic winds. Product priced at $150,000 annually for enterprise banking licenses.

competitors.title: Watershed Raises $150M Series D, Announces Financial Services Vertical
competitors.description: Watershed closed Series D at $2.5B valuation, announcing dedicated product suite for banks including financed emissions calculation and PCAF-aligned portfolio analytics. Company plans Asia expansion with Singapore office opening Q3 2026.

research.title: Climate Risk and Financial Stability in South Asia: Stress Testing Approaches for Banking Supervisors
research.description: Study models climate-induced credit losses for Indian banks at 2.3-4.1% of risk-weighted assets under a delayed transition scenario. Recommends supervisors require granular asset-level hazard exposure data from lenders by 2027.

research.title: Parametric Insurance for Agricultural Credit: Lessons from South Asian Pilots
research.description: IFC analysis of 14 parametric pilots across India and Bangladesh found 40% reduction in claim processing time and 28% lower basis risk when using hybrid triggers combining satellite indices with ground sensors. Recommends bank-insurer partnerships for distribution scale.

research.title: Sigma 2/2026: Natural Catastrophes in 2025 — Record Losses in Asia Pacific
research.description: Swiss Re reports $48 billion insured losses in Asia-Pacific for 2025, with India accounting for $7.2 billion driven by monsoon flooding and Cyclone Anika. Protection gap in India estimated at 89%, highest among major emerging markets.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Financial Flows
research.description: UNEP FI survey of 85 asset managers finds 67% cite inadequate ESG data coverage as primary barrier to increasing emerging market allocations. India ranks 4th among EMs for data availability but gaps persist in Scope 3 and physical risk metrics.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk Assessment for Indian Banking Sector
research.description: CRISIL analysis of 42 listed Indian banks estimates ₹3.2 lakh crore in loan exposures face elevated physical risk, concentrated in agriculture (38%), MSME (27%), and affordable housing (19%). Coastal and Indo-Gangetic portfolios most vulnerable.

research.title: Climate Risk Insurance: Market Development and Regulatory Enablers
research.description: BIS paper examines 28 jurisdictions' regulatory frameworks for parametric insurance, finding that explicit authorization and standardized trigger definitions accelerate market growth by 3-4 years. Highlights India's draft framework as potential regional benchmark.

ourRead.headline: RBI's binding climate risk guidelines create a 12-month window for StepChange to become the default infrastructure for Indian bank stress testing—but global competitors are already circling.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: Indian financial regulators are moving from voluntary disclosure to mandatory climate risk management, and the race to serve this market is accelerating faster than anticipated. The RBI's final guidelines, IRDAI's parametric draft, and SEBI's BRSR expansion collectively create binding compliance timelines that will force every major Indian lender to procure climate risk analytics within the next 18 months.

The RBI's March 28 guidelines are the most consequential policy development for StepChange since inception. The September 2026 deadline for climate scenario analysis from large banks creates immediate procurement urgency—these institutions will begin RFP processes within weeks, not months. StepChange's PD/LGD modelling capability maps directly to what RBI now requires: physical hazard data integrated into credit risk frameworks. The World Bank study reinforcing 2.3-4.1% potential RWA impact gives us third-party validation for client conversations.

Jupiter's HDFC partnership is a direct competitive threat that demands response. A $4-5 million annual contract with India's largest private bank signals that global climate risk firms view Indian FIs as tier-one revenue opportunities, not emerging market experiments. However, Jupiter's model—asset-level scoring for specific perils—is narrower than StepChange's integrated approach. Our positioning should emphasize regulatory alignment: RBI wants scenario analysis across portfolios, not just property-level flood scores. Meanwhile, Sprih's $18M raise to build physical risk modules shows domestic competitors are pivoting toward our core bet—we must accelerate feature development to maintain differentiation.

The CRISIL report quantifying ₹3.2 lakh crore in elevated physical risk exposure is immediately usable collateral. These figures—38% agriculture, 27% MSME, 19% affordable housing—should anchor every client presentation. The IFC parametric study provides the evidence base for our insurance bet: 40% faster claims processing and 28% lower basis risk with hybrid triggers validates our data layer's value proposition. As IRDAI moves to finalize its draft framework (likely by Q3 2026), StepChange should position to be the trigger data provider that banks and insurers require for product design.

Watch IRDAI's final parametric regulations—early indications suggest approval by July 2026, which would accelerate our insurance bet timeline by two quarters. Immediate action: the sales team should prioritize scheduling RBI compliance readiness workshops with the top 15 banks by assets before Jupiter or Moody's establishes incumbent positions. First mover in the compliance conversation becomes the default vendor.
